content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 16, 1994

Mary Lolonis
Risley & Goor
Sixteenth Floor, 800 West Sixth Street
Los Angeles, CA 90017

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-250

Dear Ms. Lolonis:

This letter confirms the oral advice given by this office on
July 27, 1944.

You stated that your organization, Americans for Death with
Dignity, is registered as a tax exempt civic organization under 26
U.S.C. § 501(c)(4). You further stated that your ballot measure
committee, of the same name, desires to terminate its political
activities. Accordingly, your committee wishes to donate its
campaign account funds to the nonprofit organization in order to
terminate the campaign account. Your committee may donate
campaign funds to that organization under Cal. Gov't Code § 89515.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Daniel E. Muallem
Counsel, Legal Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: Your committee may donate campaign funds to that organization under Cal. Gov't Code § 89515.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a ballot measure committee may donate its remaining campaign funds to a related nonprofit tax-exempt civic organization when terminating its political activities.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that the ballot measure committee may donate its campaign account funds to the nonprofit organization under California Government Code Section 89515 in order to terminate the campaign account.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document confirms prior oral advice from July 27, 1994 (document notes 1944 date but context indicates 1994). No formal Q/A sections present but question and conclusion are clearly identifiable in letter body. Minor date discrepancy in oral advice citation (1944 vs. 1994).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a ballot measure committee may donate its remaining campaign funds to a related nonprofit tax-exempt civic organization when terminating its political activities.

CONCLUSION: Your committee may donate campaign funds to that organization under Cal. Gov't Code § 89515.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms oral advice regarding Americans for Death with Dignity's ballot measure committee's ability to donate remaining campaign funds to its related tax-exempt civic organization upon terminating political activities. The donation is permitted under Cal. Gov't Code § 89515.
